current_output_problem: Generic name output does not yet feel like a usable wheel picker; it needs visible entries, selection flow, and a result state.
exact_intent_specific_features_to_add: Add an option-aware wheel experience with parsed entries, shuffle order, highlighted selected name, short celebration/result block, excluded/retry list guidance, and copy/share-ready winner text.
maximum_relevant_options: Entry source; number of entries; selection mode; remove winner yes/no; animation speed; theme; result tone.
expected_output_structure: Visual wheel/segment list, current spin result, alternate picks, copyable winner announcement, optional remaining entries list, fairness note.
per_result_actions: Copy selected name; copy full winner announcement; copy remaining entries; reshuffle/spin again.
competitor_beating_features: Feels like a decision tool rather than a plain list, supports repeat picking without duplicate winners, includes transparent fair-selection language.
safety_notes: Do not imply certified randomness or prize legality; keep usage framed for casual classroom/team/event picking.
validation_notes: Verify slug exists; verify wheel/list renders on mobile without overflow; validate copy buttons and no homepage fallback.

current_output_problem: Current output risks being a simple random name list rather than a focused picker with clear winner, alternates, and input cleanup.
exact_intent_specific_features_to_add: Parse pasted names, remove empty duplicates when requested, select a winner, show runner-up alternates, add context-aware announcement copy, and provide a compact picker summary.
maximum_relevant_options: Entry list; number of winners; allow duplicates; picker context; tone; include alternates.
expected_output_structure: Winner result card, runner-up cards, cleaned entry count, fairness note, copyable announcement, optional CSV-style result summary.
per_result_actions: Copy winner; copy announcement; copy full result; reroll selection.
competitor_beating_features: Handles messy pasted entry lists better than basic pickers and provides announcement-ready copy for meetings, classrooms, and events.
safety_notes: Avoid gambling, sweepstakes, or legal fairness claims; say users should follow their own event rules.
validation_notes: Verify duplicate handling; verify long names wrap; ensure multiple-winner output stays readable.

current_output_problem: Raffle output can be too close to generic random picking and may accidentally suggest gambling-style framing.
exact_intent_specific_features_to_add: Create fair random selection for non-gambling giveaways with entrant cleanup, winner and backup winners, draw log summary, announcement copy, and rule reminder.
maximum_relevant_options: Entrant list; number of winners; backup count; remove duplicates; draw purpose; announcement tone.
expected_output_structure: Draw summary, winner list, backup list, entrant count, copyable announcement, simple draw log, fairness/reminder note.
per_result_actions: Copy winner list; copy public announcement; copy draw log; copy remaining entrant list.
competitor_beating_features: More operational than competitors by including backup winners, entrant cleanup, and a transparent draw summary.
safety_notes: No gambling framing, no lottery/sweepstakes legal claims, no guarantee of compliance; use for casual or policy-governed draws only.
validation_notes: Verify winner count cannot exceed cleaned entrants; validate no fake certification wording; check mobile wrapping for long entrant names.

current_output_problem: Already functional but can become more useful with batches, labels, constraints, and copyable results instead of a bare number.
exact_intent_specific_features_to_add: Add batch generation, min/max awareness, unique/repeat controls, sorted/unsorted views, labeled use cases, result stats, and copyable CSV/plain formats.
maximum_relevant_options: Minimum; maximum; quantity; unique values; sort order; number format; purpose label.
expected_output_structure: Primary result numbers, compact stats, copyable list, CSV line, usage note, seed/fairness note if applicable.
per_result_actions: Copy single result; copy all numbers; copy CSV; regenerate.
competitor_beating_features: Beats simple generators by producing practical batch formats and readable stats for classroom, testing, and planning workflows.
safety_notes: Do not claim cryptographic security, gambling suitability, or certified randomness.
validation_notes: Verify range edge cases; ensure long number lists scroll/wrap; no overflow in result cards.
